description: Three photos of bermuda grass in winter dormancy in Atlanta GA. Tests that the AI correctly identifies dormancy as a healthy/expected condition, not disease or drought, and avoids recommending fertilization.

photos.caption: Wide view of uniformly tan/straw-colored bermuda lawn in winter dormancy — consistent coloring across the entire lawn with no patchy or irregular browning.
Close-up of dormant bermuda blades showing uniform straw color and dry texture consistent with cold-induced dormancy.
Edge of property showing dormant bermuda alongside a hardscape border — uniform straw color throughout with no green patches, indicating fully dormant turf.

profile.notes: Front yard, bermuda is tan and dormant for the winter as expected. No problems noted — just the normal winter color.
profile.currentRoutine: No mowing during dormancy. Minimal irrigation in winter. Will resume normal schedule when green-up begins in spring.

groundTruth.photoNotes: Three photos of bermuda in full winter dormancy. Uniform straw/tan color across the entire lawn — no irregular patches, no disease symptoms, no pest damage. Consistent coloring throughout indicates normal cold-induced dormancy rather than disease or drought injury.
